description_long: This is a sleek, modern jet airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply pointed nose, painted primarily in white with blue and black accents. The aircraft features a NASA insignia on its vertical stabilizer, indicating its use for research or training purposes. Its wings are low-mounted and swept back, with underwing pylons and no visible winglets. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, likely accommodating two seats in tandem. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with a single nose wheel and two main wheels visible.
description: A white, blue, and black NASA jet airplane with swept low wings, a pointed nose, and a tandem enclosed cockpit. Features a conventional tail and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the NASA logo on the tail, the pointed nose, and the full length of the swept low wings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin horizontal stabilizers, single vertical stabilizer, and the jet exhaust at the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the cockpit canopy and underwing pylons.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the sharp nose, cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical sweep of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: twin jet engines integrated at the rear fuselage
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: tricycle retractable landing gear with one nose wheel and two main wheels
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed tandem cockpit with a clear canopy
